summary:
The primary location for embrasures resulting from divergent contacts on proximal tooth surfaces is along the facial-ligual margins; this positioning reflects typical dental architecture without pathological alterations. \boxed{A}

The summary captures the essential point about where these embrasures are most likely located based on standard dentition structure while adhering strictly within a two-sentence limit as requested.
